event: Jesus and a companion walking toward Jerusalem along the coast
symbols: road ahead — the path of Jesus's ministry leading inexorably toward Jerusalem
two figures walking together — discipleship and companionship on the way
mountains and sea — the landscape of the Holy Land through which Jesus traveled
scroll or book carried — the Scriptures that Jesus came to fulfill
description: Two robed figures walk together along a coastal path toward the viewer. The figure on the right, wearing a white robe with a brown cloak and headcovering, carries what appears to be a scroll or book — this is Jesus. His companion on the left wears darker clothing. Behind them, a winding dirt path leads along the shore of a blue sea, with a hilltop town or city visible in the middle distance and blue-purple mountains on the horizon. The landscape is bathed in soft, warm light suggesting either morning or late afternoon. The mood is contemplative and purposeful.
theological_description: This scene captures Jesus and a companion on the road toward Jerusalem, embodying the deliberate, purposeful journey that defines the central section of Luke's Gospel: 'As the time approached for him to be taken up to heaven, Jesus resolutely set out for Jerusalem' (Luke 9:51). The Greek word translated 'resolutely' literally means He set His face like flint — echoing the Suffering Servant of Isaiah: 'I have set my face like flint, and I know I will not be put to shame' (Isaiah 50:7). Every step toward Jerusalem brings Jesus closer to the cross, yet He walks with calm determination because He knows this is the Father's will. The companion walking beside Him represents the call to discipleship — 'If anyone would come after me, let him deny himself and take up his cross daily and follow me' (Luke 9:23). The beauty of the landscape through which they travel reminds us that the Creator walks through His own creation toward the place of sacrifice. The scroll Jesus carries may represent the Scriptures He came to fulfill — 'everything must be fulfilled that is written about me in the Law of Moses, the Prophets and the Psalms' (Luke 24:44).

scripture_refs.reason: Jesus resolutely setting out for Jerusalem — the deliberate journey depicted in this scene
Setting His face like flint — the Suffering Servant's determination echoed in Jesus's purposeful walk
Following Jesus on the way of the cross — the companion walking beside Him
Everything written about Christ must be fulfilled — the scroll He carries

_meta.title: We decided to go to Jerusalem
